steps.title: Identify the researcher's central claim
steps.content: The researcher claims that some lakes have seen a “decline in the duration of ice” between the early 1980s and mid-2000s. A typical example must demonstrate this specific decline—fewer ice days in 2005-06 than in 1980-81.

steps.title: Match the lake data to the decline claim
steps.content:
Choice B — Oulujärvi, which had fewer days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980-81. shows Oulujärvi had fewer days in 2005-06 than 1980-81, directly supporting the researcher's claim.

Choices A and D present single baseline values without comparing the periods to establish decline, while Choice C — Lake Neusiedl, which had more days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980-81. contradicts the claim by asserting an increase.

That matches Choice B — Oulujärvi, which had fewer days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980-81..
